name: Canned Apple Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease 8 wide-mouth pint canning jars with melted shortening using a brush, being careful not to get grease on the jar rims.
2. Cream together the shortening and sugar.
3. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then add the applesauce.
4. Sift together the dry ingredients (baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, cloves, flour) and blend them into the batter with the applesauce mixture.
5. Stir in the raisins and pecans.
6. Divide the batter evenly between the 8 jars, filling them more than half full.
7. Bake the jars at 325 degrees F for about 60 minutes.
8. After baking, quickly remove one hot jar at a time and clean its sealing edge.
9. Immediately apply and firmly tighten a two-piece wide-mouth canning lid to each jar. The lid will form a vacuum seal as the jar cools.
10. Store cooled jars on the pantry shelf or in the freezer. The bread is safe to eat as long as the jars remain vacuum sealed and free of mold growth.
Jars of cooled bread may be stored on the pantry shelf with other canned foods or may be placed in a freezer. The bread is safe to eat as long as the jars remain vacuum sealed and free of mold growth.
